title: Chapter 2 - URLs
description: Check if you can recognize the main parts of a URL.

explanations.overview: The path is the part after the host, like `/shop` or `/users/123`.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of query parameters like `?page=2&sort=price`?

options.label: They change the domain name into an IP address.
They send extra options to the server.
They are only used to make links look longer.

explanations.overview: Query parameters are extra key/value data sent with the request.

byOptionId.b: Correct. They’re used for filters, paging, sorting, and options.
byOptionId.c: Encryption comes from HTTPS/TLS, not query parameters.
byOptionId.d: They have real meaning for the server.

questions.prompt: What does the `#section-2` part of a URL usually do?

options.label: It forces the request to use HTTP instead of HTTPS.
It changes the server’s IP address.
It becomes a password for the request.
It tells the browser to jump to a part of the page.

explanations.overview: Fragments (`#...`) are handled by the browser to navigate within a page or client-side app.

byOptionId.d: Correct. It’s often used to jump to a heading or route inside a single-page app.
byOptionId.b: IP addresses come from DNS and networking, not fragments.
byOptionId.a: Protocol changes are in the `http`/`https` part, not the fragment.

questions.prompt: Which part of a URL tells the browser which server (domain) to contact?

explanations.overview: The host (domain) tells the client which server to connect to; the path selects the resource on that server.

byOptionId.b: Correct — the host/domain is used to resolve the server via DNS.
byOptionId.c: The path is the resource on the server.
byOptionId.a: Query string passes extra options to the server.
byOptionId.d: Fragment is handled client-side and does not choose the server.
